title: Fold-safe hierarchical target encodings instead of global freq/factor codes
summary: The current best code still builds category frequencies and interaction codes on train+test combined data, which is a classic leakage/drift trap for high-cardinality tabular problems.
rationale: The current best code still builds category frequencies and interaction codes on train+test combined data, which is a classic leakage/drift trap for high-cardinality tabular problems. Regularized target encoding literature supports shrinkage-based encodings for sparse categories, and CatBoost’s docs reinforce that ordered target-derived statistics are the right inductive bias for categorical features.
implementation_hint: Replace `*_freq` and global factorized interaction IDs with race-fold-local encodings such as `Driver`, `Driver×Compound`, `Driver×Race`, `Race×Compound`, `Year×Race`, using nested shrinkage to parent levels and leave-one-race-out or forward-only computation. Keep one model with raw CatBoost cats and one model with these encodings for XGBoost/LightGBM, then compare both grouped CV and forward event-block CV.
expected_effect: Usually small but real lift from less optimistic CV and better generalization on unseen driver/race combinations; also likely reduces dependence on ad hoc adversarial weighting.
risk: If shrinkage is weak or fold generation is imperfect, this can overfit worse than the current transductive statistics and hide the issue behind inflated grouped CV.
